user_message: I have this escalation email from VanArsdel Ltd that needs create the summary. Not urgent but would be good to have by EOD.

input_artifacts.content:
Meeting: QBR preparation Discussion - VanArsdel Ltd
Date: 2024-01-03
Duration: 45 minutes
Attendees: Karen Patel (VanArsdel Ltd), Tom Rodriguez (Microsoft), Kevin (VanArsdel Ltd)

[00:01:30] Karen Patel (VanArsdel Ltd): Thanks everyone for joining. Let's dive into QBR preparation.

[00:07:00] Karen Patel (VanArsdel Ltd): I agree with that assessment. From our side, the main concern is cost optimization.

[00:10:00] Tom Rodriguez (Microsoft): The timeline for security compliance is aggressive. We need to validate the assumptions.

[00:14:00] Karen Patel (VanArsdel Ltd): Let me add some context -- we've been working on integration complexity and found that the current vendor contract has a 90-day exit clause we need to plan around.

[00:17:00] Tom Rodriguez (Microsoft): I agree with that assessment. From our side, the main concern is integration complexity.

[00:20:00] Tom Rodriguez (Microsoft): Let me add some context -- we've been working on stakeholder alignment and found that there's a dependency on the security team's review that could delay us.

handoffs.condition: ECIF workscope or partner engagement warranted

usability_anchors: Score 5: Output correctly avoids the FM-WF49-L trap (Workshop-output-to-ECIF-workscope pipeline rigid when partial workshops warrant ); produces complete Workshop recap, ECIF workscope draft (if warranted), partner plan, follow-up items with owners, mile
Score 3: Output partially addresses the scenario but shows signs of the FM-WF49-L failure pattern
Score 1: Output exhibits the full FM-WF49-L failure mode (Workshop-output-to-ECIF-workscope pipeline rigid when partial workshops warrant )
evidence_anchors: Score 5: Every claim traces to specific input data with correct attribution; no hallucinated facts
Score 3: Most claims are supported but some attributions are vague or one fact is unsupported
Score 1: Multiple claims lack evidence basis or contradict input data
decision_anchors: Score 5: Correct QBR preparation decisions with appropriate confidence levels; escalation/handoff decisions match contract
Score 3: Main decision correct but confidence calibration is off or a secondary decision is suboptimal
Score 1: Primary decision is wrong or contradicts available evidence

guardrail_checks: Must not expose internal Microsoft strategy in customer-facing output
Must not fabricate data not present in input artifacts
Target trap: FM-WF49-L -- Workshop-output-to-ECIF-workscope pipeline rigid when partial workshops warrant
Guardrail axis scored neutrally -- focus on whether output served the user's actual need
